parsed_review.summary: CP00-862 read-only review: PASS_WITH_FINDINGS. Descriptor-only RP28 marketplace permission-decision evidence bridge pack is complete, internally consistent, and matches the live plan/baseline; all no-write/no-real-data and authority boundaries (Claude read-only/not-final-approver, Hermes no runtime receipt, human approval required, no enterprise-trust claim) hold and are validated/tested. Pack honestly reports pending-review (no production_ready overclaim); no P0/P1/P2. One P3 transparency note that the full validation suite remains a post-review precondition.

findings.title: Full validation suite deferred to post-review; read-only review confirms static correctness only
findings.details: command-evidence.json records gate_outcome=pending_valid_pack_review_and_full_validation and only two initial commands executed at exit 0 (rp28:marketplace:validate and node --test packages/marketplace/test/*.test.js). The broader suite required before closeout (npm run closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, npm run validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) has not yet been executed and is explicitly deferred to after this Claude review, adjudication, and queue regeneration. As a read-only reviewer (Read/Grep/Glob, no Bash) I verified structural/static consistency of the binding, requirements, case set, descriptor, validators, tests, contract projection, and authority boundaries, but did not execute these commands. This is the expected workflow sequencing, recorded for transparency rather than as a defect.
findings.recommendation: Before committing/closing CP00-862, run the full validation command list and confirm each passes (and git diff --check is clean), then finalize adjudication and queue regeneration. Do not let the PASS verdict short-circuit the post-review validation gate.
